source_docs.title: High-throughput sequencing of the transcriptome and chromatin accessibility in the same cell

oligos.name: Beads-oligo-dT (Drop-seq barcoded bead)
oligos.role: Barcoded capture bead: oligo(dT) primer bearing shared cell barcode + UMI that captures both mRNA poly(A) and the splint-oligo poly(A)
oligos.notes: ChemGenes cat. no. Macosko201110 Drop-seq bead. Read 1 (30 bp) reads the 12-bp cell barcode + 8-bp UMI immediately 3' of the SMART handle.

oligos.name: Splint oligonucleotide (Nextera-R1-rc-polyA)
oligos.role: Splint that bridges tagmented genomic DNA to the bead: 5' region is complementary to the transposed Nextera Read-1 adapter, 3' poly(A) is captured by the bead oligo(dT)
oligos.notes: Added to Drop-seq lysis buffer at 10 uM. 5' /5Phos/ phosphate; 5' 33-nt block (CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGACGCTGCCGACGA) is the reverse complement of the Nextera Read-1 adapter, followed by a poly(A) tail.

oligos.name: Nextera-R1-bk (Read-1 blocking oligo)
oligos.role: Blocking oligo that saturates free transposed Read-1 adapter ends on the beads before RT
oligos.notes: Used at 100 uM in 6x SSC wash. 3' inverted dT block. Sequence = Nextera Read-1 handle + mosaic end.

oligos.name: Nextera-Ad2-bk (Read-2 blocking oligo)
oligos.role: Blocking oligo that saturates the free transposed Read-2/Ad2 adapter ends before RT
oligos.notes: Used at 100 uM. 3' inverted dT block. Reverse complement of the Nextera Read-2 handle GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG.

oligos.name: Template Switch Oligo (TSO)
oligos.role: Template-switching oligo adding a defined handle to the 3' end of first-strand cDNA during reverse transcription
oligos.notes: Used at 2.5 uM in the RT/ligation mix. 3' rGrGrG folded to GGG.

oligos.name: Tso-PCR (cDNA/whole-transcriptome PCR primer)
oligos.role: PCR primer annealing to the SMART/TSO handle; used in the shared amplification and alone for cDNA enrichment
oligos.notes: Shared PCR uses Nextera-R2 + Tso-PCR (16 cycles); cDNA library enriched with Tso-PCR alone.

oligos.name: Nextera-R2 (Read-2 PCR primer)
oligos.role: PCR primer that appends the Nextera Read-2 handle onto the far (Ad2) mosaic end of tagmented genomic fragments during the shared amplification

oligos.name: Nextera / Tn5 mosaic-end (ME) adapter
oligos.role: Double-stranded adapter loaded on TDE1 Tn5 transposase and inserted into accessible chromatin (Read-1 and Read-2 arms), reconstructed from the blocking oligos and Read-2 primer
oligos.notes: TDE1 Tagment DNA Enzyme in 2x Nextera Tagment DNA Buffer. Sequences grounded in Nextera-R1-bk, Nextera-Ad2-bk and Nextera-R2; standard Nextera mosaic end AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG.

oligos.name: P5XX-Tso (i5 / P5 chromatin index primer)
oligos.role: P5-side indexing PCR primer that adds the P5 adapter and an i5 sample index and primes on the bead SMART handle to build the chromatin library
oligos.notes: 3' terminal ...GAGT*A*C carries phosphorothioate bonds. Six i5 variants: P501 TAGATCGC, P502 CTCTCTAT, P503 TATCCTCT, P504 AGAGTAGA, P505 GTAAGGAG, P506 ACTGCATA. The 8-bp i5 is the 'index 2' read used for demultiplexing the chromatin library.

oligos.name: Ad2.X (i7 / P7 chromatin index primer)
oligos.role: P7-side indexing PCR primer that adds the P7 adapter and an i7 sample index and primes on the Nextera Read-2 handle to build the chromatin library
oligos.notes: Six i7 variants (index shown in name is the sample sheet index; primer carries its reverse complement): Ad2.1 TAAGGCGA->TCGCCTTA, Ad2.2 CGTACTAG->CTAGTACG, Ad2.3 AGGCAGAA->TTCTGCCT, Ad2.4 TCCTGAGC->GCTCAGGA, Ad2.5 GGACTCCT->AGGAGTCC, Ad2.6 TAGGCATG->CATGCCTA.

oligos.name: Read1CustomSeqB (custom Read-1 sequencing primer)
oligos.role: Custom Read-1 sequencing primer that primes across the cell barcode + UMI for both cDNA and chromatin libraries
oligos.notes: Primes read 1 (30 bp) on HiSeq 2500. Equals linker + SMART handle of the P5XX-Tso primer / bead.

oligos.name: HP10 (index-1 sequencing primer)
oligos.role: Custom sequencing primer used to prime index-1 (75 bp genomic read) of the chromatin library
oligos.notes: Named in Methods (Drop-seq custom primer); exact sequence not provided in the source documents.

oligos.name: HP11 (Read-2 sequencing primer)
oligos.role: Custom sequencing primer used to prime read 2 (cDNA 80 bp; chromatin 75 bp genomic read)
oligos.notes: Named in Methods (Drop-seq custom primer); exact sequence not provided in the source documents.

final_library.source_label: SNARE-seq chromatin accessibility library (final Illumina construct; cell-barcode-shared cDNA library built in parallel via Nextera XT)
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC [SAMPLE_INDEX:8] GCCTGTCCGCGG AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC [CELL_BARCODE:12] [UMI:8] TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG [CDNA] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC [SAMPLE_INDEX:8] ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG
annotation_lines: TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG = Nextera Read 1 / Tn5 mosaic end
CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC = Nextera Read 2 / Tn5 mosaic end (rev comp)

evidence.locator: SNARE-seq chromatin accessibility library (final Illumina construct; cell-barcode-shared cDNA library built in parallel via Nextera XT)

library_generation.title: Tn5 tagmentation of permeabilized nuclei
library_generation.summary: TDE1 Tn5 fragments accessible chromatin in intact nuclei and inserts Nextera mosaic-end adapters while remaining bound to hold the fragments together.
library_generation.note: ~200,000 nuclei tagmented with TDE1 in 2x Nextera Tagment DNA Buffer + RNase inhibitor, 37 C, 30 min. Without heat/detergent the transposase stays bound, preserving fragment contiguity.
library_generation.product:
5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[gDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC -3'
3'- AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC[gDNA]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG -5'
(Tn5 still bound; 9-bp gaps at each mosaic end)

library_generation.title: Droplet co-encapsulation + splint/mRNA capture
library_generation.summary: Nuclei, barcoded beads and splint oligos are co-droplet-encapsulated; heat lysis releases fragments and the splint bridges each genomic fragment to the bead while mRNA poly(A) is also captured.
library_generation.note: 72 C, 5 min releases Tn5 and nuclei contents inside droplets. Splint 5' end anneals to the transposed Read-1 adapter; splint 3' poly(A) and mRNA poly(A) both hybridize to the bead oligo(dT).
library_generation.product:
Bead   |--5'-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC[CELL_BARCODE:12][UMI:8]TTTTTTTTTT...T-3'
                                                              ::::::::::::  (dT:dA)
Splint                                    3'-AAAAAAAAAA...AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACAC...-5'
(splint 5' region anneals to genomic TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG adapter)

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription / template switching + barcode ligation
library_generation.summary: One droplet reaction reverse-transcribes mRNA with template switching and links the cell barcode to genomic fragments via T7 ligase and Klentaq.
library_generation.note: RT/ligation mix (reverse transcriptase, TSO, Hemo Klentaq, T7 ligase) at RT 30 min then 42 C 90 min. RNA: dT-primed first-strand cDNA gains the TSO handle. Chromatin: bead oligo is extended/ligated across the splint to carry the barcode onto the genomic fragment.

library_generation.title: Exonuclease I cleanup
library_generation.summary: Exonuclease I removes unextended single-stranded oligos from the beads.

library_generation.title: Shared amplification PCR (Nextera-R2 / Tso-PCR)
library_generation.summary: A single 16-cycle PCR co-amplifies barcoded cDNA and barcoded genomic fragments, appending the Nextera Read-2 handle to the genomic Ad2 end.
library_generation.note: Primer pair Nextera-R2 + Tso-PCR, 16 cycles, on ~10,000 beads. Tso-PCR primes the SMART handle; Nextera-R2 primes the distal mosaic end.
library_generation.product:
5'- AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC[CELL_BARCODE:12][UMI:8](T)30 TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[gDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC -3'
(cDNA amplicon flanked by SMART/TSO handles amplified in parallel)

library_generation.title: cDNA branch: enrichment + Nextera XT
library_generation.summary: Half the shared PCR product is enriched with Tso-PCR alone and converted to a standard cDNA library with a Nextera XT kit.
library_generation.note: 0.6x bead size-selection, Tso-PCR-only enrichment, second 0.6x selection, then Nextera XT tagmentation/indexing (Drop-seq style).

library_generation.title: Chromatin branch: indexing PCR (P5XX-Tso / Ad2.X)
library_generation.summary: The other half is amplified with P5XX-Tso and Ad2.X to add i5/P5 and i7/P7, yielding the final size-selected chromatin accessibility library.
library_generation.note: P5XX-Tso primes the SMART handle to add P5+i5; Ad2.X primes the Read-2 handle to add P7+i7. 225-1,000 bp fragments are excised from a PAGE gel and column-purified.

library_sequencing.note: cDNA library on Illumina HiSeq 2500. Primer anneals to the SMART/bead handle on the bottom strand and reads the shared 12-bp cell barcode + 8-bp UMI (barcode/UMI taken from the first 20 bases).
Read 2 (80 bp) primed by HP11 on the Nextera Read-2 handle; reads into the cDNA insert. Aligned to hg38/mm10 after trimming SMART adapter and poly(A).
Chromatin library on HiSeq 2500. Same shared 12-bp cell barcode + 8-bp UMI read as the cDNA library, priming on the bead SMART handle.

Genomic ATAC read (75 bp), termed 'index 1' in the Methods but is one of the paired open-chromatin mates. Primes on the transposed Nextera Read-1 / mosaic end and reads into the accessible genomic insert. Processed with the ENCODE ATAC-seq pipeline.
Second paired genomic ATAC mate (75 bp), primed by HP11 on the Nextera Read-2 / Ad2 mosaic end, reading into the accessible insert from the opposite side.
8-bp index-2 read of the P50X i5 sample index used to demultiplex the chromatin library; primes 3' of the i5 (in the linker/SMART region) and reads the index.

title: SNARE-seq
description: SNARE-seq (Single-Nucleus chromatin Accessibility and mRNA Expression sequencing) is a droplet-based dual-omics assay that jointly profiles open chromatin and the transcriptome from the same individual nuclei. Accessible sites in permeabilized nuclei are tagmented by Tn5 (Nextera adapters) before droplet generation; inside Drop-seq droplets a splint oligo whose 5' end is complementary to the transposed Read-1 adapter and whose 3' polyA is captured by oligo(dT) beads links each genomic fragment to the same cellular barcode that captures the cell's polyadenylated mRNA. Reverse transcription with template switching builds the RNA library while T7 ligase/Klentaq attach the barcode to genomic fragments, yielding paired cDNA and chromatin libraries that share a cell barcode and are read out on Illumina.

reference.label: High-throughput sequencing of the transcriptome and chromatin accessibility in the same cell

original_publication.title: High-throughput sequencing of the transcriptome and chromatin accessibility in the same cell

throughput.summary: Droplet dual-omics: paired transcriptome + chromatin accessibility profiles from single nuclei. 1,047 and 1,043 human cell-line-mix nuclei; 5,081 neonatal (P0, n=5) and 10,309 adult (n=12) mouse cerebral cortex nuclei.
throughput.cells: 5,081 (P0 mouse cortex) and 10,309 (adult mouse cortex) paired nuclei; 1,047 / 1,043 human cell-line-mix nuclei
throughput.rna: Median UMIs: 500 (cell mix), 357 (P0), 1,159 (EZ cell mix), 1,332 (adult); 66% RNA recovery
throughput.dna: Median accessible sites: 2,720 (GM12878), 805/2,254 (cell mix), 2,583 (P0), 2,000 (adult); 100% chromatin recovery; ~6% doublet rate

publication.statistical_model: Negative binomial modeling of read counts with cell-size-factor normalization (Monocle); latent Dirichlet allocation topic modeling of chromatin accessibility (cisTopic); Fisher's exact test for differential accessibility

other.label: Platform
other.value: Drop-seq microdroplet (ChemGenes Macosko201110 beads); Illumina HiSeq 2500

other.label: Key innovation
other.value: Splint oligo (Nextera-R1-rc-polyA) links Tn5-tagmented accessible DNA to oligo(dT) beads for joint capture with mRNA

data_processing.summary: Paired-end reads are split by library and linked through the shared cell barcode. RNA reads are quality-filtered, barcode/UMI-demultiplexed (first 20 bases), trimmed of SMART adapter and poly(A), aligned to hg38/mm10 with STAR, and collapsed into a UMI expression matrix; the paired genomic (chromatin) reads carrying the same barcode are run through the ENCODE ATAC-seq pipeline for peak calling and a per-cell accessibility matrix. Expression is clustered with PAGODA2/Seurat, chromatin is topic-modeled with cisTopic, and the linked modalities are used for differential-accessibility (Fisher's exact) and Monocle pseudotime analyses.
data_processing.statistical_model: Negative binomial with cell-size-factor normalization (Monocle); latent Dirichlet allocation topic modeling (cisTopic); Fisher's exact test for differential accessibility.
